title: Abacopteris simplicifolia

Traditionally, Abacopteris simplicifolia has been used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, alkaloids, and sesquiterpenes, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. taxonomy: Abacopteris simplicifolia is classified as a species of fern in the family Thelypteridaceae. This family of ferns is a part of the larger division Pteridophyta, which includes all ferns and their allies. The genus Abacopteris is further grouped within the subfamily Thelypteridoideae, indicating its relationship to other ferns in the Thelypteridaceae family. Abacopteris simplicifolia is native to certain regions in Africa. Its classification provides valuable information about its evolutionary history and relationships with other plant species.
